# **Bug \#1**

| Field | Details |
| ----- | ----- |
| Bug ID | BUG-001 |
| Title | Habit Saves to DB But Does Not Appear on Dashboard (Silent Display Failure) |
| Severity | High |
| Priority | P1 |
| Status | Open |
| Reported By | Ranque, Christian Ville M. |
| Assigned To | Joshua Serrano |
| Date Found | 03/11/2026 |
| Date Fixed | 03/13/2026 |
| GitHub Issue \# | N/A |

## **Description**

After completing the Create Habit flow and selecting CREATE HABIT, the Dashboard incorrectly displays “No habits for this selection.” even though the habit was successfully saved. This is confirmed when the user tries to create the same habit again and receives the “Already Exist” dialog. The root cause is in DashboardController.getHabitsWithLogs(), where the query excludes habits whose related daily\_log entry has isCompleted \= \-1. Since newly created habits may be assigned this value when created after the valid time period has already ended, the condition (l.isCompleted IS NULL OR l.isCompleted \!= \-1) filters them out, causing the Dashboard to fail to display the habit on the same day it was created.

## **Steps to Reproduce**

1. Launch the app and complete onboarding.  
2. Tap the \+ button on the Dashboard to open the Create Habit screen.  
3. Fill in a valid habit title, select a Focus Area, choose an icon and color, then tap Save.

## **Expected vs Actual**

Expected: The habit is inserted into the habits table in SQLite and immediately appears as a card on the Dashboard.

Actual: The habit is not saved. The Dashboard does not show the new habit after tapping Save.

## **Screenshots / Evidence**

![Screenshots / Evidence](../assets/screenshots/evidence_3.png)

## **Environment**

Device: **Android Phone**  |  OS: **Android 14**  |  Flutter: **Flutter 3.41.4**

# **Bug \#2**

| Field | Details |
| ----- | ----- |
| **Bug ID** | BUG-007 |
| **Title** | Completed Habit Cannot Be Deleted or Retired — Tap Blocked by "Already Completed" Snackbar |
| **Severity** | High |
| **Priority** | P1 |
| **Status** | Open |
| **Reported By** | Ranque, Christian Ville M. |
| **Assigned To** | Joshua Serrano |
| **Date Found** | 03/9/2026 |
| **Date Fixed** | 03/10/2026 |
| **GitHub Issue \#** | N/A |

## **Description**

Once a habit is marked as done (isCompleted \= true), tapping on its tile in the Dashboard no longer opens the habit actions bottom sheet. Instead, the code in \_buildHabitTile() in dashboard\_screen.dart checks if isDone is true and immediately shows a snackbar: "This habit is already completed." — then returns without calling \_showHabitActions(). This completely blocks the user from accessing Edit Configuration, Skip for Today, and End Habit Series (the only way to delete/retire a habit) for any habit that has been completed today. The user is permanently locked out of managing a completed habit until the next day when its completion status resets.

## **Steps to Reproduce**

4. Create a habit and return to the Dashboard.  
5. Tap MARK AS DONE on the habit card to log it as completed for today.  
6. Observe the habit tile becomes dimmed (opacity 0.4) and moves to the DAILY LOGS section.  
7. Tap on the completed habit tile to try to access the options menu (Edit, Skip, Delete).  
8. Observe the result — the options bottom sheet does not open.

## **Expected vs Actual**

Expected: Tapping a completed habit opens the habit actions bottom sheet with options: Edit Configuration, Skip for Today, and End Habit Series — allowing the user to delete or retire the habit regardless of its completion status.

Actual: A snackbar appears at the bottom of the screen: "This habit is already completed." The habit actions bottom sheet never opens. The user has no way to delete or retire a completed habit on the same day it was completed.

## **Screenshots / Evidence**

![Screenshots / Evidence](../assets/screenshots/evidence_4.png)

## **Environment**

Device: **Android Phone**  |  OS: **Android 14**  |  Flutter: **Flutter 3.41.4**
